summary: Live-applied and verified the repo-managed Plane service for ADR 0193 across the Proxmox host, postgres-lv3, docker-runtime-lv3, and nginx-lv3. The Plane controller endpoint, governed repo wrappers, full ADR synchronization flow, full playbook replay, and authenticated public tasks.example.com edge path all passed from this branch.

verification.check: Focused Plane and edge regression tests
verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest pytest -q tests/test_nginx_edge_publication_role.py tests/test_plane_client.py tests/test_plane_runtime_role.py` passed with 27 tests after adding Plane timeout retries, no-op issue update skips, sync timeout budget hardening, and the shared webroot ACME defaults.

verification.check: Plane syntax and targeted repo validation
verification.observed: `make syntax-check-plane` passed, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards json health-probes workstream-surfaces alert-rules` passed after recording the generated dependency-graph surface in the workstream ownership manifest.

`ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING=False ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/plane.yml --private-key /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e env=production -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump --tags tier-2 --limit docker-runtime-lv3` completed successfully with `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=118 changed=5 unreachable=0 failed=0`.
`http://100.64.0.1:8011/api/instances/` returned `200 OK` with Plane instance metadata and `workspaces_exist: true`.

`make plane-manage ACTION=whoami` returned `ops@example.com` on workspace `lv3-platform`, and `make plane-manage ACTION=list-projects PLANE_ARGS='--workspace lv3-platform'` returned the `Architecture Decisions` project with identifier `ADR`.
`make plane-manage ACTION=sync-adrs` completed successfully and returned `count: 202`, including live issue `ADR 0193: Plane Kanban Task Board` under external id `adr-0193`.

`ANSIBLE_HOST_KEY_CHECKING=False ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/plane.yml --private-key /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e env=production -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump` completed successfully across `proxmox-host`, `postgres-lv3`, `docker-runtime-lv3`, and `nginx-lv3`. The Plane ADR sync task retried once and then converged.
`curl -k -I https://tasks.example.com/` returned `302` to `/oauth2/sign_in`, and probing that sign-in URL returned `302` into the shared `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3/...` realm flow.

`make validate` completed the generated-vars checks, full syntax suite, YAML lint, role argument specs, idempotency policy, preflight second-run checks, shell lint, JSON validation, compose runtime env guard, and retry guard, but still exited non-zero on pre-existing unrelated `ansible-lint` warnings in `control_plane_recovery`, `monitoring_vm`, `openbao_runtime`, and `windmill_runtime`.

notes: The shared NGINX edge certificate automation now defaults to the repo-managed `webroot` ACME path on `nginx-lv3`, which avoided the previous `_acme-challenge.tasks.example.com` DNS-01 propagation failure.
The governed Plane sync wrapper now uses a larger timeout budget and retry window so slow Plane PATCH responses can still converge through `make plane-manage ACTION=sync-adrs`.
This workstream branch intentionally leaves the protected integration files (`README.md`, `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, and `versions/stack.yaml`) for the final merge-to-main step.
